zh.Musbury, Devonshire, England: 英國英格蘭戴文姆斯波利
zh.lifeSummary: 當Ralph Fitz Robert de Drake於1210年出生在英國戴文時，他的父親Robert Fitzhumphrey De Mount Drake45歲，母親Christian Fawcett53歲。他至少有1個兒子。他去世於1249年在英國英格蘭戴文姆斯波利享年39歲。

nameMeanings.name: Drake
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English drake, either ‘drake, male duck’ (compare Duck ) or ‘dragon’ (Old English draca ‘snake, dragon’ or the cognate Old Norse draki), including an emblematic dragon on a flag (compare Dragon ). Both the Old English and the Old Norse forms are from Latin draco ‘snake, monster’; its sense as a nickname is unclear but it may have had the sense ‘standard bearer’. The name was taken to Ireland in the 13th century and reinforced by later English settlers in the 17th century.
German: from Low German drake ‘dragon’, familiar as image on signboards, hence a topographic or habitational name referring to a house or inn with such signboard.
Dutch: variant, mostly Americanized and Flemish, of Draak, a cognate of 2 above, from draak (Middle Dutch drake) ‘dragon’.

variants.name: Duck
meanings: Irish: adoption of the English name (see 1 above) as an equivalent of Lohan (an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Leocháin ‘descendant of Leochán’) by mistranslation, as if from lacha ‘duck’.
North German (also Dück) and German (Dück): see Dueck .

variants.name: Drakes
meanings: English: variant of Drake , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

English: habitational name from Drax (Yorkshire). The placename probably derives from Old English dræg ‘portage, place where boats are dragged’ or dræg ‘drag net’, influenced by Old Norse drag ‘portage’, with a plural drages being shortened to drags, Drax.
In some cases perhaps an Americanized form of some similar (like-sounding) cognate in some other language.

nameMeanings.name: Ralph
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Norman French name, Raulf, a contracted form of the Germanic personal name Radulf, derived from rād ‘counsel’ + wulf ‘wolf’. The spelling with -ph is due to classical influence in the 18th century.

variants.name: Ralf
meanings: From a Norman French name, Raulf, a contracted form of the Germanic personal name Radulf, derived from rād ‘counsel’ + wulf ‘wolf’. The spelling with -ph is due to classical influence in the 18th century.

nameMeanings.name: Fitz
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Either a short form of any of the numerous names representing a transferred use of a surname formed with the Anglo-Norman French prefix fi(t)z ‘son of ’, as for example Fitzgerald , Fitzroy , or a transferred use of the surname Fitz, which may be of the same derivation, but applied originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same given name, or a local name from Fitz in Shropshire, named with the Old English personal name Fitt + hōh ‘hill spur’.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: When Ralph Fitz Robert de Drake was born in 1210, in Devon, England, his father, Robert Fitzhumphrey De Mount Drake, was 45 and his mother, Christian Fawcett, was 53. He had at least 1 son. He died in 1249, in Musbury, Devon, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 39.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Ralph Fitz Robert de Drake est né en 1210, à Devon, Angleterre, son père, Robert Fitzhumphrey De Mount Drake, avait 45 ans et sa mère, Christian Fawcett, avait 53 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en 1249, à Musbury, Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 39 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Ralph Fitz Robert de Drake 1210 in Devon, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Robert Fitzhumphrey De Mount Drake 45 und seine Mutter Christian Fawcett 53. Er hatte mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1249 im Alter von 39 Jahren in Musbury, Devon, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Ralph Fitz Robert de Drake è nato il nel 1210 nel Devon, England, il suo padre, Robert Fitzhumphrey De Mount Drake, aveva 45 anni e il suo madre, Christian Fawcett, aveva 53 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1249, nel Musbury, Devon, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 39 anni.

ja.Musbury, Devonshire, England: イギリスイギリスデボンムスブリー
ja.lifeSummary: Ralph Fitz Robert de Drakeが1210年にイギリスデボンで生まれた時，父Robert Fitzhumphrey De Mount Drakeは45歳，母Christian Fawcettは53歳であった。彼は少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は1249年にイギリスイギリスデボンムスブリーで39歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Ralph Fitz Robert de Drake(이)가 1210년 영국 데폰에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert Fitzhumphrey De Mount Drake(은)는 45세였고, 그의 어머니 Christian Fawcett(은)는 53세였다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1249년 영국 잉글랜드 데폰 무스버이에서 39세의 나이로 사망했다.

mn.Musbury, Devonshire, England: Musbury, Devon, Англи, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Ralph Fitz Robert de Drake nasceu em 1210, em Devon, Inglaterra, seu pai, Robert Fitzhumphrey De Mount Drake, tinha 45 anos e sua mãe, Christian Fawcett, tinha 53 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 1249, em Musbury, Devon, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 39 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Ralph Fitz Robert de Drake родился в 1210 г. в местности девон, Англия, его отцу по имени Robert Fitzhumphrey De Mount Drake было 45 лет, а матери, которую звали Christian Fawcett, 53. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в 1249 г. в местности мусбури, девон, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 39 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Ralph Fitz Robert de Drake nació en 1210, en Devon, Inglaterra, su papá, Robert Fitzhumphrey De Mount Drake, tenía 45 años y su mamá, Christian Fawcett, tenía 53 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1249, en Musbury, Devon, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 39 años.
